entries.purpose: Tunes the advanced parameters of AetherSDR's client-side noise-reduction engines (NR2, NR4, MNR, DFNR), letting operators dial in the tradeoff between noise suppression and speech fidelity.
entries.how_to_open: Settings > AetherDSP Settings...

controls.label: Gain Method
controls.behavior: Selects gain-curve mapping used by NR2.
controls.notes: Stored as integer 0-3 matching the order above.

controls.label: Smoothing:
controls.behavior: Controls how smoothly the noise estimate tracks changes.

controls.label: Noise Estimation Method
controls.behavior: Selects noise-floor estimator used by NR4.

controls.label: Adaptive Noise Estimation
controls.behavior: Enables continuous re-estimation of the noise floor.

controls.label: Reduction (dB):
controls.behavior: Sets maximum NR4 noise reduction in dB.

controls.label: Smoothing (%):
controls.behavior: Time-domain smoothing of NR4 noise estimate.

controls.label: Reset Defaults
controls.behavior: Restores NR4 defaults (SPP-MMSE, adaptive on, 10 dB, 0, 0, 0.50, 0.50).

controls.label: Enable MNR (macOS only)
controls.behavior: Enables MMSE-Wiener noise reduction with asymmetric gain smoothing.
controls.notes: Initial state read live from AudioEngine::mnrEnabled().

controls.label: RN2 (tab)
controls.behavior: Selects the RN2 (RNNoise) page -- purely informational, no adjustable parameters.

controls.label: BNR (tab)
controls.behavior: Selects the BNR (NVIDIA) page -- intensity controlled from the overlay menu.

controls.label: Attenuation Limit
controls.behavior: Sets maximum noise attenuation applied by DeepFilterNet3.

controls.label: Post-Filter Beta
controls.behavior: Applies an additional post-filter for extra suppression.

related_user_tasks: Tune NR2 reduction depth and voice threshold
Switch NR2 gain method between Linear, Log, Gamma and Trained
Change NR2 noise power estimator (OSMS/MMSE/NSTAT)
Adjust NR4 reduction amount in dB
Enable or disable NR4 adaptive noise estimation
Tune NR4 masking depth and suppression strength
Enable MNR on macOS and set its strength
Set DeepFilterNet3 attenuation limit for strong or weak signals
Configure DFNR post-filter beta for extra suppression
Reset NR2 or NR4 parameters to defaults

entries.purpose: Central hub for connecting to DX spot sources -- DX cluster, Reverse Beacon Network, WSJT-X, SpotCollector, POTA and FreeDV -- and configuring how spots are displayed on the panadapter.
entries.how_to_open: Settings > SpotHub...

controls.label: Cluster (tab)
controls.behavior: Shows DX cluster telnet connection, console and spot-color picker.

controls.label: Server:
controls.behavior: Hostname of DX cluster to connect to.

controls.label: Connect / Disconnect
controls.behavior: Toggles telnet connection to the cluster.

controls.label: Cluster Console
controls.behavior: Read-only telnet console of raw cluster traffic.

controls.label: Send (cluster command line)
controls.behavior: Sends a typed command to the cluster.

controls.label: Spot Color:
controls.behavior: Opens a color picker for cluster spots.

controls.label: RBN (tab)
controls.behavior: Reverse Beacon Network telnet source with rate limiting.

controls.label: WSJT-X (tab)
controls.behavior: WSJT-X UDP listener plus filters and colors.

controls.label: Address:
controls.behavior: UDP bind address for WSJT-X messages.

controls.label: CQ
controls.behavior: Show only CQ calls from WSJT-X.

controls.label: Calling Me
controls.behavior: Show only decodes addressed to your callsign.

controls.label: CQ color / POTA color / Calling Me color / Default color
controls.behavior: Color pickers for each WSJT-X spot category.

controls.label: Spot Life:
controls.behavior: Seconds WSJT-X spots remain on panadapter.

controls.label: SpotCollector (tab)
controls.behavior: UDP listener for Ham Radio Deluxe SpotCollector broadcasts.

controls.label: FreeDV (tab)
controls.behavior: WebSocket feed of FreeDV QSO reporter spots.

controls.label: Start / Stop (FreeDV)
controls.behavior: Connects or disconnects the FreeDV WebSocket.

controls.label: Spot List (tab)
controls.behavior: Unified searchable table of all live spots.

controls.label: Bands:
controls.behavior: Per-band checkboxes toggle visibility in table.

controls.label: Spot table
controls.behavior: Sortable table of spots. Double-click row to tune.
controls.notes: Columns: Time, Freq, DX Call, Comment, Spotter, Band, Mode, Source.

controls.label: Auto Mode:
controls.behavior: Auto-selects spot density based on zoom.

controls.label: Levels:
controls.behavior: Number of vertical stacking rows for spots.

controls.label: Spot Lifetime:
controls.behavior: Seconds before a spot fades away.

controls.label: Override Colors:
controls.behavior: Forces a single text color for all spots.

controls.label: Override Background: Enabled / Auto
controls.behavior: Enables custom spot background and auto-contrast mode.

controls.label: Log File (ADIF):
controls.behavior: Loads an ADIF log file to drive DXCC coloring.

controls.label: Auto-Reload Log:
controls.behavior: Re-reads ADIF file when it changes.

indicators.label: Status (Cluster/RBN/WSJT-X/SpotCollector/POTA/FreeDV)
indicators.meaning: Current connection/listener state for each source.

indicators.label: Total spots count
indicators.meaning: Total spots currently tracked across all sources.

indicators.label: DXCC stats
indicators.meaning: Imported QSO count from ADIF log when DXCC coloring is enabled.

related_user_tasks: Connect to a DX cluster
Connect to the Reverse Beacon Network
Start WSJT-X UDP listener and filter for CQ, POTA or calls to me
Enable SpotCollector UDP feed
Poll POTA activations
Enable FreeDV QSO reporter WebSocket
Pick colors for each spot source
Clear all spots from the panadapter
Tune to a spot by double-clicking the spot list
Enable DXCC coloring from an ADIF log
Tune spot density, position, font size and lifetime
Auto-reload ADIF log when updated by a logger

entries.purpose: Displays bundled Markdown help topics (getting started, noise cancellation, data modes, etc.) in a styled offline reader.
entries.how_to_open: Help > Getting Started... / AetherSDR Help... / Understanding Noise Cancellation... / Configuring AetherSDR Controls... / Configuring Data Modes... / Contributing to AetherSDR...

controls.label: AETHERSDR OFFLINE HELP (eyebrow)
controls.behavior: Brand header shown above the title.

related_user_tasks: Open bundled getting-started guide
Read the full AetherSDR help document
Learn the differences between NR2, NR4, DFNR and MNR
Configure digital modes step-by-step
Understand how to contribute bug reports and PRs

entries.purpose: Manages the radio's memory channels -- add, edit, search, filter by profile, tune, import/export and delete stored frequencies.
entries.how_to_open: Settings > Memory...

controls.label: Search:
controls.behavior: Filters the table by memory name.

controls.label: Memory table
controls.behavior: Displays and edits memory rows. Columns: Group, Owner, Frequency, Name, Mode, Step, FM TX Offset Dir, Repeater Offset, Tone Mode, Tone Value, Squelch, Squelch Level, RX Filter Low, RX Filter High, RTTY Mark, RTTY Shift, DIGL Offset, DIGU Offset.

controls.label: Export...
controls.behavior: Exports selected memories to a file.

controls.label: Add
controls.behavior: Creates a new memory at the current VFO.

controls.label: Edit
controls.behavior: Enters inline-edit mode on selected memory.

controls.label: Tune
controls.behavior: Tunes the active slice to the selected memory.

indicators.label: Selection count
indicators.meaning: Shows '<N> selected' total rows selected.

related_user_tasks: Add a memory at current frequency
Edit a memory's name, mode or offset inline
Tune the radio to a stored memory
Delete one or more memories
Search memories by name
Filter memories by profile
Import memories from a CSV/JSON file
Export memories for backup or sharing
Sort memory table by column header

entries.purpose: Configures a MIDI controller: select the device, record bindings with Learn mode, tune per-binding behavior, and save/load mapping profiles.
entries.how_to_open: Settings > MIDI Mapping...

controls.label: Category
controls.behavior: Filters parameter combo to a control category.

controls.label: Parameter
controls.behavior: Chooses the target parameter for a new binding.

controls.label: Learn
controls.behavior: Starts listening for the next MIDI message and binds it to the selected parameter.

controls.label: Bindings table
controls.behavior: Shows existing bindings with per-row Invert, Relative and delete controls.
controls.notes: Columns: Parameter, MIDI Source, Channel, Invert, Relative, (delete).

controls.label: Invert
controls.behavior: Reverses the control direction for the row.

controls.label: Relative
controls.behavior: Treats the control as an endless encoder.

controls.label: Profile:
controls.behavior: Picks a saved MIDI mapping profile.

controls.label: Save
controls.behavior: Saves current bindings as a profile.

indicators.label: Port status
indicators.meaning: Shows whether the MIDI port is currently open.

indicators.label: Activity indicator
indicators.meaning: Shows most recent MIDI message received.

related_user_tasks: Connect a MIDI controller
Record a new binding with Learn mode
Invert a knob or treat it as an endless encoder
Delete a binding
Save the current mapping as a named profile
Load a previously saved MIDI profile
Auto-connect MIDI controller on startup

entries.purpose: Shows every station currently sharing the FLEX radio and lets the operator grant local PTT or release control.
entries.how_to_open: Settings > multiFLEX...

controls.label: Enable
controls.behavior: Enables or disables multiFLEX on the radio.

controls.label: Stations table
controls.notes: Columns: LOCAL PTT, STATION, TX ANT, TX FREQ (MHz).

controls.label: Enable (PTT)
controls.behavior: Toggles local PTT authority for this station.

indicators.label: Local PTT label
indicators.meaning: Shows which station currently holds PTT.

related_user_tasks: See all stations connected to this FLEX
Grant or revoke local PTT
Check which antenna and frequency each TX station is using
Enable multiFLEX on the radio

entries.purpose: Live view of the network link to the radio: TCP/UDP endpoints, RTT, per-category data rates and drops, audio buffer health and jitter.
entries.how_to_open: Settings > Network...

indicators.label: Status
indicators.meaning: Overall link state.

indicators.label: Target Radio IP
indicators.meaning: IP of connected radio.

indicators.label: Selected Source
indicators.meaning: Local NIC/bind path used for the connection.

indicators.label: Local TCP
indicators.meaning: Local TCP endpoint.

indicators.label: Local UDP
indicators.meaning: Local UDP endpoint.

indicators.label: First UDP Packet
indicators.meaning: Time of first UDP packet received.

indicators.label: Latency (RTT)
indicators.meaning: Current round-trip time.

indicators.label: Max Latency (RTT)
indicators.meaning: Highest RTT seen since connect.

indicators.label: Audio / FFT / Waterfall / Meters / DAX rates
indicators.meaning: Per-category ingress rate.

indicators.label: Total RX / Total TX
indicators.meaning: Aggregate bytes per second in each direction.

indicators.label: Audio / FFT / Waterfall / Meters / DAX drops
indicators.meaning: Per-category dropped-packet counts.

indicators.label: RX Buffer Now / Peak
indicators.meaning: Current and peak audio buffer fill.

indicators.label: Underruns (total / last sec)
indicators.meaning: Audio underrun counters.

indicators.label: Audio Arrival Gap / Max Arrival Gap
indicators.meaning: Inter-packet arrival timing.

indicators.label: Jitter Estimate
indicators.meaning: Smoothed jitter of audio stream.

related_user_tasks: Verify the radio's IP and local bind address
Measure RTT and packet drops during audio problems
Check per-category data rates (audio, FFT, waterfall, meters, DAX)
Diagnose audio underruns and jitter
Watch the first-UDP-packet timestamp after connect

entries.purpose: Picks how many panadapters are shown and how they are arranged (single, side-by-side, stacked, 2x2, 3+2, 2x3, 4+3, 2x4).
entries.how_to_open: Right-click on panadapter area (triggered by MainWindow context menu)

controls.label: Layout buttons
controls.behavior: Click the preview tile matching the desired arrangement. Confirming closes the dialog.
controls.notes: Each layout has a label showing pan count, e.g. 'A|B / C (3 pans)'.

controls.label: Cancel
controls.behavior: Closes the dialog without changing layout.

related_user_tasks: Switch to a single full-width panadapter
Split panadapter area side-by-side
Arrange a 2x2 grid of panadapters
Preview and pick among the 10 layout variants

entries.purpose: Creates, renames, loads and deletes Global, Transmit and Microphone profiles on the radio, and toggles auto-saving of transmit changes.
entries.how_to_open: Profiles > Profile Manager...

controls.label: Profile name
controls.behavior: Name used when saving a new profile.

controls.label: Profile list
controls.behavior: All profiles for this category; active one highlighted.

controls.label: Load
controls.behavior: Loads the selected profile onto the radio.

controls.label: Save
controls.behavior: Saves the current radio state under the typed name.

controls.label: Auto-save profile changes
controls.behavior: When enabled, TX changes are written back to the active profile automatically.

related_user_tasks: Save the current radio state as a new global profile
Switch to a saved transmit profile
Rename or delete a microphone profile
Turn on auto-save so TX tweaks always persist
Review the list of active global profiles
Create a separate mic profile per microphone

entries.purpose: At-a-glance HF/VHF propagation dashboard -- current solar indices, 3-day Kp forecast with blackout/radiation risk, HF day/night band conditions, solar and lunar imagery, and sporadic-E/aurora hints.
entries.how_to_open: View > Propagation Conditions (shown when the panadapter overlay is clicked) or opened programmatically from the propagation status bar.

controls.label: Current Conditions cards
controls.behavior: Five metric tiles (SFI, SN, A-index, K-index, X-ray) with hover tooltips.

controls.label: 3-Day Forecast grid
controls.behavior: Shows Kp per 3-hour UTC period for each of three days, plus Max Kp, R1-R2, R3+ and S1+ risk rows.

controls.label: Solar And Lunar panel
controls.behavior: Live solar image (click cycles wavelengths) and current Moon phase.

controls.label: What To Look For
controls.behavior: Rotating plain-language learning notes about the current solar image.

controls.label: HF Band Conditions
controls.behavior: Day and night condition per band row.

controls.label: VHF Conditions
controls.behavior: Aurora, E-Skip NA, E-Skip EU states.

controls.label: What These Mean (VHF)
controls.behavior: Two learning notes explaining aurora vs sporadic-E.

indicators.label: Metric cards
indicators.meaning: Show current solar/geomagnetic indices with plain-language detail text.

indicators.label: Kp cells (3 x 8)
indicators.meaning: Color-coded Kp forecast across UTC periods.

indicators.label: R1-R2 / R3+ / S1+
indicators.meaning: NOAA blackout and radiation storm risk per day.

indicators.label: Geomagnetic field / Solar wind / Noise / X-ray
indicators.meaning: Summary labels under the forecast grid.

indicators.label: Aurora / E-Skip NA / E-Skip EU
indicators.meaning: Current VHF propagation openings.

indicators.label: Rationale
indicators.meaning: Plain-language explanation of today's forecast.

related_user_tasks: Check current solar flux, sunspot number and K-index
See the 3-day Kp forecast and blackout risk
Decide which HF band is open for day or night work
Watch for VHF sporadic-E or auroral openings
Cycle solar imagery wavelengths to build intuition
Read rotating learning notes about solar conditions

entries.purpose: Master per-radio configuration window -- radio info, network, GPS, TX, Phone/CW, RX, audio, filters, XVTR, USB cables, peripherals and (optionally) FlexControl serial.
entries.how_to_open: Settings > Radio Setup... (also via Settings > FlexControl..., Settings > USB Cables..., which jump to the matching tab)

controls.label: Radio (tab)
controls.behavior: Radio information, identification, license info and firmware update.

controls.label: Station Name
controls.behavior: Station name stored on the radio.

controls.label: License Info (Subscription / Expiration / Radio ID / Licensed version)
controls.behavior: Displays license details from the radio.

controls.label: Upload Firmware
controls.behavior: Starts firmware upload with progress bar and status.

controls.label: Network (tab)
controls.behavior: Radio network information and advanced network options.

controls.label: DHCP / Static
controls.behavior: Switches between DHCP and Static IP modes.

controls.label: Apply
controls.behavior: Pushes the network config to the radio.

controls.label: GPS (tab)
controls.behavior: GPS presence and live lat/lon/alt/time/satellites info.

controls.label: TX (tab)
controls.behavior: TX timings, interlocks, max power, tune mode, waterfall display, slice/TX follow and TX Band Settings shortcut.

controls.label: TX Band Settings
controls.behavior: Opens the dedicated per-band power/tune dialog.

controls.label: Interlocks - TX REQ: RCA / Accessory
controls.behavior: Enables RCA and accessory interlock inputs.

controls.label: Tune Mode:
controls.behavior: Selects how the tune button behaves.

controls.label: Show TX in Waterfall:
controls.behavior: Draws TX signal in the waterfall.

controls.label: TX Follows Active Slice / Active Slice Follows TX
Enable/Disable the Level Meter During Receive
controls.behavior: Shows mic level meter even in RX.

controls.label: RX (tab)
controls.behavior: GPSDO frequency offset calibration and 10 MHz reference source.

controls.label: 10 MHz Reference Source:
controls.behavior: Selects internal or external 10 MHz reference.

controls.label: Audio (tab)
controls.behavior: Radio audio outputs, compression, PC devices, boost, buffer, recording and NVIDIA BNR container.

controls.label: Prevent system sleep while connected
controls.behavior: Keeps OS awake while radio is connected.

controls.label: Audio Boost:
controls.behavior: Enables extra gain on the client audio path.

controls.label: Idle timeout:
controls.behavior: Seconds of silence before recording stops.

controls.label: NVIDIA BNR: Autostart Container / Start / Stop / Check Status
controls.behavior: Controls the NVIDIA Broadcast noise-removal container.

controls.label: Filters (tab)
controls.behavior: Low-latency / Sharp filter options per bandwidth.

controls.label: Use Low Latency Filters for Digital Modes
XVTR (tab)
controls.behavior: Per-transverter configuration -- RX Only, valid, remove, plus Create New Transverter.
controls.notes: Contains nested tabs, one per xvtr, and a '+' tab.

controls.label: USB Cables (tab)
controls.behavior: Assigns USB serial adapters to CAT, BCD, bit, and PTT cable types.

controls.label: Cables list / Status
controls.behavior: Detected USB cables per type with Plugged/Unplugged status.

controls.label: Name: / Enabled / Speed / Data Bits / Parity / Stop Bits / Flow / Source / Auto Report / BCD Type / Polarity / Bit Configuration (0-7)
Peripherals (tab)
controls.behavior: External devices manual IP connection (TGXL, PGXL, Antenna Genius).

controls.label: Serial (tab)
controls.behavior: FlexControl serial port selection, baud/data/parity/stop, pin function assignment (DTR/RTS/CTS/DSR), paddle swap, auto-open, and button action mapping.

controls.label: Auto-open serial port on startup
controls.behavior: Reopens the port on app launch.

controls.label: FlexControl Tuning Knob: Detect / Close
controls.behavior: Detects or closes a FlexControl knob.

controls.label: Auto-detect on startup / Invert tuning direction

indicators.label: Firmware status
indicators.meaning: Empty until a firmware upload begins, then progress and result text.

indicators.label: NVIDIA BNR status dot
indicators.meaning: Colored dot indicating container Running/Stopped/Unknown.

related_user_tasks: Set the radio nickname, callsign and station name
Check radio serial, hardware version, region and options
Upload a new firmware .ssdr to the radio
Switch the radio between DHCP and static IP
Change network MTU for VPN/remote setups
Calibrate the GPSDO frequency offset
Switch to an external 10 MHz reference
Set per-band TX max power and tune mode
Choose PC input/output audio devices
Pick Opus vs uncompressed audio for SmartLink
Turn on audio boost or enlarge the audio buffer for remote operation
Enable auto-record on TX and pick a save folder
Start/stop the NVIDIA BNR container
Toggle low-latency vs sharp filters per bandwidth
Create a new transverter entry
Assign a USB cable as CAT, BCD, bit or PTT
Connect TGXL, PGXL or Antenna Genius by IP
Configure FlexControl serial port pin functions

entries.purpose: Visual keyboard-shortcut editor -- click any key on the keyboard map, pick an action, and see every action in a filterable table.
entries.how_to_open: View > Configure Shortcuts...

controls.label: Keyboard map
controls.behavior: Visual QWERTY layout; click a key to select it.

controls.label: Action:
controls.behavior: Assigns an action to the selected key.

controls.label: Category
controls.behavior: Shows the category of the selected action.

controls.label: Clear
controls.behavior: Removes the assignment from the selected key.

controls.label: Reset to Default
controls.behavior: Restores the default key for the selected action.

controls.label: Filter:
controls.behavior: Filters the action table by text.

controls.label: Category:
controls.behavior: Filters the action table by category.

controls.label: Action table
controls.behavior: All actions. Columns: Action, Category, Current Key, Default Key. Select a row to edit.

controls.label: Reset All to Defaults
controls.behavior: Resets every binding to its default key.

indicators.label: Capturing key state
indicators.meaning: When true, the next keypress will be captured as the new binding.

related_user_tasks: Rebind a keyboard shortcut
Find all actions in a category
Reset one shortcut to its default key
Reset every shortcut back to defaults
See the default key for any action

entries.purpose: Captures a JSON snapshot of every slice and summarizes likely problems (missing audio, stuck mute, missing antenna) so users can share it with support.
entries.how_to_open: Help > Slice Troubleshooting...

controls.label: Issue Summary (tab)
controls.behavior: Plain-language bullet list of detected problems.

controls.label: JSON (tab)
controls.behavior: Full JSON snapshot of slices and DAX channels.

controls.label: Refresh Snapshot
controls.behavior: Re-reads slice state into the snapshot.

controls.label: Copy Summary
controls.behavior: Copies the issue summary to the clipboard.

controls.label: Copy JSON
controls.behavior: Copies the full JSON to the clipboard.

controls.label: Export JSON...
controls.behavior: Saves the JSON to a file.

indicators.label: Status label
indicators.meaning: Shows last copy/export result (e.g. 'Copied to clipboard').

related_user_tasks: Capture a slice snapshot for support
Read a plain-language list of suspected slice problems
Copy the full JSON snapshot to the clipboard
Export the snapshot to a file to attach to a bug report
Refresh the snapshot after changing slice state

entries.purpose: Quick stand-alone settings window for how DX spots and memories render on the panadapter -- toggles, density, position, font, lifetime, and color overrides.
entries.how_to_open: Opened from panadapter context menu / Spots overlay (not in main menu).

controls.label: Spots:
controls.behavior: Master toggle for DX spot display.

controls.label: Spot Lifetime:
controls.behavior: How long spots remain before fading.

controls.label: Override Colors:
controls.behavior: Forces a single text color for all spots.

controls.label: Spot text color picker
controls.behavior: Opens QColorDialog to pick text color.

controls.label: Override Background: Enabled
controls.behavior: Draws a background under spot text.

controls.label: Clear All Spots
controls.behavior: Clears all spots from the panadapter.

indicators.label: Total Spots:
indicators.meaning: Shows count of live spots currently tracked.

related_user_tasks: Turn spots on or off
Overlay memory channels on the panadapter
Change spot density and vertical position
Enlarge or shrink the spot font
Shorten or lengthen spot lifetime
Force a single spot text color
Pick a custom background color for spots
Adjust spot background opacity
Clear every spot from the panadapter

entries.purpose: Log viewer and support tools -- toggle logging categories, view/clear the live log, open the log folder, reset settings, and start an AI-assisted bug report.
entries.how_to_open: Help > Support...

controls.label: Category checkboxes
controls.behavior: Per-category log enable/disable, one row per category.

controls.label: Log path label
controls.behavior: Shows the current log file path.

controls.label: Log viewer
controls.behavior: Scrollable view of the most recent log text.

controls.label: Open Log Folder
controls.behavior: Opens the log directory in the OS file browser.

controls.label: Reset Settings
controls.behavior: Resets AetherSDR settings to defaults (with confirmation).

controls.label: File an Issue
controls.behavior: Launches the AI-Assisted Bug Report flow.

indicators.label: Log file size
indicators.meaning: Current size of the active log file.

related_user_tasks: Enable verbose logging for a specific subsystem
View the live log without leaving the app
Clear the log before reproducing a bug
Open the log folder to grab multiple files
File an AI-assisted bug report
Reset AetherSDR settings to factory defaults

entries.purpose: First-launch and on-demand release-notes viewer. Shows changes between the last seen version and the current version as styled HTML.
entries.how_to_open: Help > What's New... (also shown automatically on first launch after a version change)

controls.label: Release notes browser
controls.behavior: Scrollable HTML view of release entries.

controls.label: Got it — 73!
controls.behavior: Dismisses the dialog and marks the version as seen.

controls.label: Upgrade
controls.behavior: Shown only when an upgrade is available; opens download page.

controls.label: Skip this version
controls.behavior: Skips release nag for this version.

related_user_tasks: Read what changed in the new version
Skip the current version's release notes
Open the upgrade flow for a newer build

entries.purpose: Novice-first connection screen that lets the user pick between a local LAN radio, a SmartLink remote radio, or a manual/VPN IP connection.
entries.how_to_open: Appears in the MainWindow central area before a radio is connected, and whenever the user disconnects.

controls.label: Local / SmartLink / Manual (mode buttons)
controls.behavior: Switches the main page among the three connection modes.
controls.notes: Implemented as QCommandLinkButtons in a QButtonGroup driving a QStackedWidget.

controls.label: Available radios
controls.behavior: Lists LAN radios discovered via mDNS/Flex discovery.

controls.label: Connect Selected Radio
controls.behavior: Connects to the highlighted LAN radio.

controls.label: No local radios found yet
controls.behavior: Callout shown when discovery is empty.

controls.label: Remote radios
controls.behavior: Lists SmartLink WAN radios available to the account.

controls.label: Connect Remote Radio
controls.behavior: Starts a WAN connection to the selected radio.

controls.label: Network Diagnostics
controls.behavior: Opens NetworkDiagnosticsDialog from the Manual page.

controls.label: Advanced: Source path
controls.behavior: Picks local NIC used for the manual connection.

controls.label: Use low bandwidth mode
controls.behavior: Enables reduced-rate streams for slow links.

indicators.label: Status label
indicators.meaning: Current connection state (searching / connecting / connected / errored).

indicators.label: Manual result label
indicators.meaning: Result text after probing a manual IP (success or error).

indicators.label: Source warning label
indicators.meaning: Warns when the selected source NIC is stale or unreachable.

related_user_tasks: Connect to a local LAN radio
Retry discovery when no radios appear
Log in to SmartLink to see remote radios
Connect to a remote radio through SmartLink
Connect by IP across a VPN or routed network
Pick the local network interface used for a manual connection
Enable low-bandwidth mode for slow links
Disconnect from the current radio

entries.purpose: Vertical strip of color-coded frequency bookmarks that live beside the panadapter -- click to recall, right-click to delete, '+' to bookmark the current tuning.
entries.how_to_open: Sits alongside each panadapter in the MainWindow splitter; always visible when a radio is connected.

controls.label: Bookmark buttons
controls.behavior: Click to tune the panadapter to the stored frequency; right-click to delete.
controls.notes: Color reflects the band-plan segment for that frequency.

controls.behavior: Adds a new bookmark at the active slice's frequency.

related_user_tasks: Bookmark the current frequency
Recall a stored bookmark with one click
Delete a bookmark you no longer need
Visually scan the stored frequencies for the active band

entries.purpose: CWX operating panel -- send a typed buffer live, watch a scrolling send history, speed control, and a setup view with 12 F-key macros plus QSK.
entries.how_to_open: Appears in the MainWindow central area when the active slice uses CW/CWL/CWU mode or when the user opens the CWX feature.

controls.label: Send (view)
controls.behavior: Shows the live send area with history and text field.

controls.label: Setup (view)
controls.behavior: Shows the macro editor and QSK setup.

controls.label: Send history scroll
controls.behavior: Shows previous send buffers with character highlighting.

controls.label: Send text area
controls.behavior: Type CW characters; Enter sends the buffer.

controls.label: F1 … F12 (macros)
controls.behavior: Sends the pre-written macro for that function key.

controls.label: F1 … F12 macro editors
controls.behavior: Setup view editors for each macro.

controls.label: Prosigns legend
controls.behavior: Shows shortcuts for common CW prosigns (=, +, (, &, $).

related_user_tasks: Send a typed CW buffer live
Trigger a CW macro with F1–F12
Edit a CW macro string
Change CW send speed in WPM
Enable QSK full break-in
Look up the prosign character shortcuts

entries.purpose: Records and plays back up to 8 voice-keyer slots on the radio; lets the user rename slots, upload WAVs, record new audio, and trigger via F-keys.
entries.how_to_open: Appears in the MainWindow central area when DVK is enabled / a voice mode is active.

controls.label: F1 … F8 slot buttons
controls.behavior: Selects and plays that slot; right-click to rename or upload a WAV.

controls.label: Slot name labels
controls.behavior: Shows the name of each slot.

controls.label: ● REC
controls.behavior: Starts recording into the selected slot.

controls.label: ◀ PREV
controls.behavior: Previews the slot through the local speaker.

controls.label: Rename edit
controls.behavior: Inline rename of a slot triggered via context menu.

indicators.label: Status: Idle / Recording / Playing
indicators.meaning: Current DVK state.

related_user_tasks: Record a new DVK slot
Play a stored voice-keyer slot
Preview a slot without transmitting
Rename a slot
Upload a WAV file into a slot
Stop a playback in progress

entries.purpose: Read-only side panel that lists stored memories next to the panadapter and lets the user jump to the memory closest to the current tuning.
entries.how_to_open: Sits as a side panel in the MainWindow splitter when memory browsing is enabled.

controls.label: Memory table
controls.behavior: Shows each memory; double-click or Enter to activate.

controls.label: No memories are available yet.
controls.behavior: Shown when the radio has no memories loaded.

indicators.label: Highlighted row
indicators.meaning: Closest memory to the current tuned frequency is highlighted.

related_user_tasks: Browse the radio's stored memories
Jump to the memory closest to the current frequency
Activate a memory with a single double-click
